data.title: StoneCo cut to Neutral at Goldman Sachs ahead of Q1 earnings release
data.description:
StoneCo (STNE) was trading lower as Goldman Sachs cut its recommendation on the stock ahead of the first-quarter earnings release.

The Brazilian payment technology company is set to release its Q1 2026 financial results on Thursday, May 14, after the market closes.

Shares were 4.63% lower at $11.12 during morning trading on Wednesday.

"We downgrade shares of Stone to Neutral from Buy as we expect weaker earnings momentum (-1% yoy in 2026), given TPV (Total Payment Volume) underperformance relative to the industry and to main peer PAGS (PagSeguro Digital), leading to market share losses," said Goldman Sachs analysts.

"We note that PAGS screens more favorably during 2026 due to an easier comp base as it recovers from weak trends in 2025," added the research note.

"Moreover, while we acknowledge that STNE has de-rated this year (12-month fwd P/E falling 7% YTD), we see limited room for multiple expansion, given the deceleration in results (we see 6% downside to Visible Alpha Consensus 2026E net income)," said the research note.

"Furthermore, we note that STNE is now trading without one of its main catalysts: the R$3bn excess cash from the Linx divestiture (closed in February 2026), which will be distributed as extraordinary dividends on May 4th," said the research note.

Goldman Sachs' rating aligns with Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating of Hold. The sell-side analysts as well as SA authors grade STNE as Buy.

data.title: Warner Bros. gains on report European antitrust regulator may clear Paramount Skydance deal
data.description:
Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) edged higher by 0.7% amid a report that European antitrust regulators are leaning toward approving the $110 billion sale to Paramount Skydance (PSKY). 

The European Commission appears to be skeptical of opponents' rationale against a transaction, according to traders, who cited a Capitol Forum report that was circulating on Wednesday. The  EC may clear the deal during its Phase 1 probe rather than from an in-depth inquiry of the deal.

The transaction is still in pre-notification phase in Europe and it's not clear when the filing will be made, though the companies are leaning towards late May or early June, according to the report.

Paramount Skydance (PSKY) expects to secure antitrust approvals comfortably in Europe to buy Warner Bros, (WBD) and would consider divestments if necessary, Reuters reported in late February.

Earlier this week, Paramount Skydance (PSKY)  flagged 49.5% foreign ownership to fund Warner Bros. *WBD) acquisition in an FCC filing.

data.title: Robinhood stock breaks below key trend line after earnings miss
data.description:
Robinhood Markets (HOOD) shares fell sharply in Tuesday morning trading after the company's first-quarter results fell short of Wall Street expectations, breaking below a key level of technical support (now resistance).

The trading platform's stock tumbled about 14% to ~$71 per share at press time, trailing its 50-day moving average around $76.39 a share, according to the chart.

Its Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.38, missing the average analyst estimate of $0.39, fell from $0.66 in Q4 2025 and increased from $0.37 in Q1 2025. Q3 net revenue of $1.07B, trailing the $1.14B consensus, declined from $1.28B in the previous quarter and increased from $927M in the year-ago quarter.

In addition to missing consensus estimates, Robinhood (HOOD) increased its guidance for full-year adjusted operating expenses and share-based compensation to include investments in Trump Accounts.

Seeking Alpha's Quant rating system ranks HOOD a Sell, while the average SA analyst and average Wall Street analyst both rate the stock a Buy.

data.title: Ten ETFs to watch as NXP Semiconductors surges 25%
data.description:
Exchange traded funds with significant exposure to NXP Semiconductors (NXPI) drew renewed investor attention after the chipmaker’s shares surged 25% on Wednesday, following stronger-than-expected first-quarter results and an upbeat outlook.

The rally followed earnings that exceeded Wall Street forecasts, alongside guidance that signaled improving demand trends across key end markets. Analysts responded with a wave of bullish commentary, further amplifying momentum in the stock and lifting sentiment across semiconductor-linked funds.

NXPI is widely held across the ETF landscape, with 312 funds collectively owning 46.9M shares. The sharp move in the stock translated into notable gains for ETFs with heavier portfolio weightings in the company, underscoring how single-stock performance can meaningfully influence fund returns.

Outlined below are the 10 ETFs that have the largest portfolio allocation towards shares of NXPI:

  1. Cambiar Aggressive Value ETF (CAMX), 4.75% allocation.
  2. Fidelity Electric Vehicles and Future Transportation ETF (FDRV), 4.49% allocation.
  3. VanEck Morningstar Wide Moat Value ETF (MVAL), 3.51% allocation. 
  4. Direxion Daily Semiconductor Bull 3x Shares (SOXL), 3.31% allocation. 
  5. Tema International Durable Quality ETF (ITOL), 3.31% allocation. 
  6. iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX), 3.21% allocation. 
  7. John Hancock Disciplined Value Select ETF (JDVL), 3.18% allocation. 
  8. First Trust Nasdaq Semiconductor ETF (FTXL), 3.10% allocation. 
  9. YieldMax Semiconductor Portfolio Option Income ETF (CHPY), 3.09% allocation. 
  10. Guinness Atkinson Funds SmartETFs Sustainable Energy II Fund (SOLR), 3.06% allocation. 

Other Semiconductor ETFs: (SMH), (XSD), (USD), (PSI), and (SEMI).

data.title: Bloom Energy soars to all-time high after routing Q1 estimates, hiking full-year guidance
data.description:
Bloom Energy (BE) up 23.8% in early trading Wednesday to an all-time high of $290.50 after crushing estimates for Q1 adjusted earnings and revenue, capitalizing on rising demand for digital power as part of the artificial intelligence boom, and raising guidance for the full year.

The fuel cell technology provider swung to a Q1 profit of $70.7M, or $0.23/share, from a loss of $23.8M, or $0.10/share, in the year-earlier quarter, and revenue more than doubled to $751.1M from $326M in the same period last year.

Bloom (BE) raised guidance for FY 2026, now seeing adjusted EPS of $1.85-$2.25 and revenue of $3.4B-$3.8B, exceeding respective FactSet consensus estimates of $1.42/share and $3.24B, after previously forecasting earnings of $1.33-$1.48/share and revenue of $3.1B-$3.3B.

Alternative energy peers also trade higher: FuelCell Energy (FCEL) up 12.4%, Plug Power (PLUG) up 2.6%, and Ballard Power (BLDP) up 0.8%.

The Q1 results came a day after Oracle and BorderPlex Digital Assets said they will use fuel cells made by Bloom Energy (BE) for Oracle’s Project Jupiter data center campus in New Mexico.

Morgan Stanley analyst David Arcaro, who rates Bloom (BE) at Overweight, raised his stock price target to $310 from $184, seeing the guidance raise as attractive with revenue and profit set to increase and margins continuing to expand.

"We believe the confirmation of the recent Oracle deal likely contributed to the increase, with at least 1.2 GW being delivered over 2026 and 2027, and the company also suggested broad-based strength across data center and C&I end markets."

data.title: Bristol-Myers Squibb Q1 Preview: Growth portfolio's performance in focus
data.description:
Bristol-Myers Squibb (BMY) is projected to report a fall in both earnings and revenue during its first-quarter results on April 30.

The consensus EPS Estimate is $1.42, translating to a 21.1% fall, while revenue is likely to see a 2.5% dip to $10.92B. Over the last 3 months, EPS estimates have seen 4 upward revisions and 7 downward revisions, while revenue estimates have seen 7 upward revisions and 1 downward move.

Ahead of the earnings, Seeking Alpha analyst Terry Chrisomalis assigned a Strong Buy rating to the stock, driven by robust Growth Portfolio expansion and strategic pipeline execution.

Heading into this quarter’s earnings, Chrisomalis noted a positive shift in the company’s momentum as revenue continues to transition from its Legacy Portfolio to its Growth Portfolio.

“The thing is that it is near having 60% of its growth portfolio as part of its total revenues, and thus I believe it is on the right track to achieving its strategic goal of countering the loss of patents for key products,” he said.

The pharma giant beat estimates last quarter as its growth portfolio offset a slump in demand for its legacy products.

Over the last 2 years, BMY has beaten EPS and revenue estimates 100% of the time.

The stock has advanced nearly 8% in the year so far, ahead of the S&P 500 index’s 4.8% rise.

data.title: Blackstone spins out AI portfolio into new unit - report
data.description:
Blackstone (BX) is forming a new West Coast-based unit, Blackstone N1, which will focus on the company's artificial intelligence portfolio, Bloomberg News reported on Wednesday.

The New York-based alternative asset manager's AI portfolio includes OpenAI (OPENAI) and Anthropic (ANTHRO), the report noted.

The unit is expected to back AI and high-growth tech bets and serve as a centralized resource on AI investing for other units, an internal memo seen by Bloomberg noted.

Veteran executive Jas Khaira will reportedly lead Blackstone N1, and relocate to San Francisco from New York for the new role.

The move reportedly marks the second by Blackstone since 2023, when the company consolidated its credit and insurance units into a single business.
